meta.title: Landforms & Bodies of Water
meta.subtitle: Mountains, Rivers, Oceans, and More • Printable Web Lesson

hero.ariaLabel: Island, mountains, and water showing landforms and bodies of water
hero.imageQuery: mountains river ocean coast island elementary geography landforms

heroCopy.lede: Earth has many natural features. Landforms and bodies of water help us describe what places look like and how people live there.
heroCopy.whatStudentsLearn: Students identify common landforms and bodies of water, compare features such as mountains and valleys, and explain how coasts and islands shape human choices.
learningTargets: I can define landform and body of water.
I can compare mountains, hills, valleys, and plains.
I can describe rivers, lakes, oceans, coasts, and islands.
I can use geography vocabulary to design an imaginary place.
studentDirections: Read the section and study the image before answering.
Use the vocabulary and quick facts to support your thinking.
Write in complete sentences unless the prompt says bullets are okay.
On paper: circle, label, or underline important evidence in the print image.

keyVocabulary.def: A natural shape on Earth’s surface.
A water feature such as a river, lake, or ocean.
The area where land meets the ocean.
Land that has water all around it.

sections.imageQuery: sea coast hill natural landform elementary geography
sections.title: What Is a Landform?
sections.lede: A landform is a natural shape on Earth’s surface. Some landforms rise high, some are flat, and some are surrounded by water.

cards.title: Quick Facts
items: Mountains rise high above nearby land.
Plains are broad and mostly flat.
Deserts, valleys, hills, and islands are landforms too.

cards.title: Look Closely
items: Find high land and low land.
Look for where land meets water.
Think about what might be hard or easy to cross.

prompt.label: Prompt A — Name the Landform
prompt.placeholder: Choose one landform and describe what it looks like.
prompt.teacherKey: ✔ Key idea: Landforms include mountains, hills, plains, valleys, islands, coasts, and deserts.

sections.imageQuery: hills river islands valley aerial geography landforms
sections.title: Mountains, Hills, and Valleys
sections.lede: Some land rises high, and some land dips low between higher places. Comparing shapes helps geographers describe a place.

cards.title: Quick Facts
items: A mountain is high and often steep.
A hill is raised land that is usually lower than a mountain.
A valley is lower land between hills or mountains.

cards.title: Compare
items: Which feature is highest?
Which feature is lowest?
Where might water flow?

sections.callout: Landforms can affect roads, weather, farms, and where people build communities.

prompt.label: Prompt B — Compare
prompt.placeholder: How are a mountain and a valley different?
prompt.teacherKey: ✔ Key idea: Mountains rise high; valleys are lower land between hills or mountains.

sections.imageQuery: river lake ocean body of water geography elementary lesson
sections.title: Rivers, Lakes, and Oceans
sections.lede: Bodies of water can be small or huge, still or moving, fresh or salty. They provide resources and travel routes.

cards.title: Vocabulary
items: River: moving water that flows across land.
Lake: water surrounded by land.
Ocean: a huge body of salty water.

cards.title: Image Mission
items: Look for moving water or still water.
Notice nearby landforms.
Ask how people might use this water.

prompt.label: Prompt C — Water Clues
prompt.placeholder: Explain how a river, lake, and ocean are different.
prompt.teacherKey: ✔ Key idea: Rivers flow, lakes are surrounded by land, and oceans are huge bodies of salty water.

sections.imageQuery: river island water around land geography elementary
sections.title: Coasts and Islands
sections.lede: A coast is where land meets the ocean, and an island has water around it. People plan carefully in these places.

cards.title: Examples
items: Coasts may have beaches, cliffs, ports, or wetlands.
Islands can be large or small.
Weather and waves can change coastal places.

cards.title: Think Like a Geographer
items: How would people travel here?
Where would buildings be safest?
What resources might people use?

prompt.label: Prompt D — Coast or Island?
prompt.placeholder: Describe a coast or island and name one challenge people might plan for there.
prompt.teacherKey: ✔ Key idea: Students connect land/water features to waves, storms, bridges, boats, or safe building.

sections.imageQuery: mountain island water geography model landforms
sections.title: Quick Check + Build a Model
sections.lede: Landforms and water features help us describe real places and imagine new ones.

cards.title: Mini Quiz
items: Define landform.
Name two bodies of water.
What makes an island an island?

cards.title: Challenge
items: Invent an island.
Add three landforms and two bodies of water.
Explain how people could travel or find water there.

promptE.label: Prompt E — Summary
promptE.placeholder: Summarize four landforms or bodies of water from this lesson.
promptE.teacherKey: ✔ Key idea: Summary accurately names and describes at least four features.

promptF.label: Prompt F — Create
promptF.placeholder: Draw or describe an imaginary island with three landforms and two bodies of water.
promptF.teacherKey: ✔ Key idea: Student includes correct geography vocabulary and labels.
